vulnerability.name: CVE-2021-44228
vulnerability.description: Apache Log4j2 JNDI features do not protect against attacker controlled LDAP and other JNDI related endpoints.

statements.impact_statement: Product Alpha does not include log4j-core library. It uses logback as the logging framework.
Product Beta uses a custom-patched version of log4j that removes the vulnerable JNDI lookup functionality.
While log4j-core is present, the vulnerable code path is never executed due to application architecture that disables JNDI lookups at startup.
statements.status_notes: CVSS: MEDIUM (5.9) | CWEs: CWE-20, CWE-674 | NVD: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2021-45105

statements.impact_statement: The vulnerable configuration parameters are hardcoded and cannot be influenced by external input or adversary control.
statements.status_notes: CVSS: MEDIUM (6.6) | CWEs: CWE-20, CWE-74 | NVD: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2021-44832

statements.impact_statement: Product Epsilon has built-in input validation and sanitization that prevents exploitation of this vulnerability. Additionally, the application runs with restricted permissions.

statements.action_statement: Update to a different revision or release and There is a workaround available
statements.status_notes: Critical vulnerability affecting all versions prior to 1.0.1. Patch available at https://acme.com/security/patches/CVE-2024-00001. Workaround: Disable remote access feature until patch is applied. | CVSS: CRITICAL (10.0) | NVD: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2024-00001

Critical vulnerability affecting all versions prior to 1.0.1. Patch available at https://acme.com/security/patches/CVE-2024-00001. Workaround: Disable remote access feature until patch is applied. | CVSS: CRITICAL (10.0) | NVD: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2024-00001

statements.impact_statement: This CVE was incorrectly associated with Product Iota. The vulnerable code does not exist in any version of our product. Confirmed false positive with NVD.
statements.status_notes: Note: This was identified as a false positive in the original assessment. | CVSS: LOW (3.1) | NVD: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2024-00004
